description: The nodev mount option can be used to prevent device files from being created in /var/tmp. Legitimate character and block devices should not exist within temporary directories like /var/tmp. Add the nodev option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /var/tmp.
rationale:
The only legitimate location for device files is the <tt>/dev</tt> directory
located on the root partition. The only exception to this is chroot jails.
ocil_clause: the "/var/tmp" file system does not have the "nodev" option set
ocil:
Verify the nodev option is configured for the /var/tmp mount point, run the following command: 
$ sudo mount | grep '\s/var/tmp\s'
. . . /var/tmp . . . nodev . . .
fixtext: Modify "/etc/fstab" to use the "nodev" option on the "/var/tmp" directory.
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must mount /var/tmp with the nodev option.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must mount /var/tmp with the nodev option.
stig.vuldiscussion:
The "nodev" mount option causes the system to not interpret character or block special devices. Executing character or block special devices from untrusted file systems increases the opportunity for nonprivileged users to attain unauthorized administrative access.

The only legitimate location for device files is the "/dev" directory located on the root partition, with the exception of chroot jails if implemented.
stig.checktext:
Verify "/var/tmp" is mounted with the "nodev" option:

$ mount | grep /var/tmp

/dev/mapper/rhel-var-tmp on /var/tmp type xfs (rw,nodev,nosuid,noexec,seclabel)

If the "/var/tmp" file system is mounted without the "nodev" option, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext: Modify "/etc/fstab" to use the "nodev" option on the "/var/tmp" directory.

title: Add nodev Option to /var/tmp
